channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Major international law firm reaches deal with White House, becoming the latest to do so
item.description: Another major international law firm has reached a deal with President Donald Trump to dedicate at least $100 million in free legal services to causes such as supporting veterans and combating antisemitism.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump to deliver 'Liberation Day' announcement from Rose Garden flanked by Cabinet members
item.description: President Donald Trump is expected to unveil his Liberation Day tariff plan in the White House's Rose Garden Wednesday in an effort to end decades-long reliance on goods made overseas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News Politics Newsletter: Tonight's elections are a Trump, Musk referendum
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump administration, Capitol Hill and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Yale professor leaving US: Trump launching 'brutal attack' on freedom
item.description: A professor at Yale University said Monday he would be leaving his post and the country over the Trump administration’s “brutal attack” on free speech.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court seems ready to let religious groups opt out of unemployment compensation laws
item.description: The case was brought by a chapter of Catholic Charities in Wisconsin, which says that it should be able to opt out of the mandatory state unemployment compensation system.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg, MPR News

item.title: White House says it’s ’case closed’ on the Signal group chat review
item.description: Last week, the White House said the National Security Council, the White House counsel office and President Trump adviser Elon Musk were all looking into the mishap.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez, MPR News

item.title: Hundreds protest the ICE detainment of a U of M graduate student
item.description: Protesters gathered at the University of Minnesota Twin Cities campus after an international graduate student was detained by immigration officials on Thursday. During the rally, it was announced that a student at Minnesota State University, Mankato was also detained.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini, Matt Sepic, and Aleesa Kuznetsov, MPR News

item.title: Mass layoffs are underway at the nation's public health agencies
item.description: Employees across the massive U.S. Health and Human Services Department received notices Tuesday that their jobs were being eliminated, part of a sweeping overhaul designed to vastly shrink the agencies responsible for protecting and promoting Americans’ health.
item.dc:creator: Carla K. Johnson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Crime, housing and Cuomo in focus in race for NYC mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Candidates on the campaign trail for New York City mayor are zeroing in on crime and affordability, as a new accusation against former Gov. Andrew Cuomo bubbles to the surface.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: What to know about your rights when crossing the border
item.description: U.S. citizens may face questions if they're traveling with visa holders or living with individuals who are in the U.S. illegally, Nasseri says.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Other states should work on banning food dyes: WV state senator
item.description: This ban will start for meals served in school nutrition programs starting this August and go statewide starting on Jan. 1, 2028.
item.dc:creator: Marni Hughes, NewsNation

item.title: US auto companies can eat costs of tariffs on parts: Trump
item.description: President Trump's senior trade adviser, Peter Navarro, says the tariffs plan will raise more than $6 trillion in federal revenue over the next decade.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: 60 percent disapprove of Trump tariffs: Survey
item.description: Just 38 percent of respondents said they approve of Trump's approach to trade negotiations with other countries.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, NewsNation

item.title: White House to remove magnolia tree dating to Andrew Jackson
item.description: Trump added that White House staff will preserve the Magnolia tree’s “Historic wood,” which, Trump said, “may be used for other high and noble purposes!!!”
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Trump tariff tumult has ripples for sporting goods, puts costly hockey gear in price-hike crosshair
item.description: The sports equipment industry is no exception to the headaches being caused throughout the U.S. commercial ecosystem by the promise of 25 percent tariffs on Canadian imports by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Catholic Charities tests Wisconsin’s unemployment payment system at Supreme Court
item.description: A chapter of Catholic Charities in Wisconsin contends it should be exempted from the state’s unemployment compensation system because it’s a charitable organization with a religious mission.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg, MPR News

item.title: 6 things borrowers should know about federal student loans right now
item.description: NPR has spent the past few weeks catching up with student loan experts and asking the Trump administration for clarity on some of borrowers’ biggest questions.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner, MPR News

item.title: FCC chair opens investigation into Disney and ABC over DEI practices
item.description: FCC chair Brendan Carr said he wants a review all of Disney's DEI practices — both past and present — to determine whether the company has ever violated any FCC regulations.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim, MPR News

item.title: Wisconsin's attorney general asks the state Supreme Court to stop Musk's $1 million payments
item.description: Wisconsin’s attorney general has asked the liberal-controlled state Supreme Court to stop billionaire Elon Musk from handing over $1 million checks to two voters. The request comes hours before President Donald Trump’s ally plans the giveaway at an evening rally in Green Bay.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Red Lake Nation leader condemns Trump orders during State of the Band Address
item.description: During the Red Lake Nation State of the Band Address Friday, chair Darrell Seki Jr., condemned President Donald Trump’s recent executive orders, saying they have created a crisis throughout Indian Country.
item.dc:creator: Mathew Holding Eagle III, MPR News

item.title: California governor candidate Chad Bianco says he'd abolish the state income tax if elected
item.description: Citing a desire to “restore the California Dream for all,” a Republican candidate in the 2026 race to become California’s next governor says he’d eliminate the state’s income tax if elected.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, Fox 5

item.title: California Assembly Democrats announce plans to leave X over disinformation, hate speech
item.description: California Assembly Democrats announced plans to leave X over disinformation, lack of content moderation and hate speech.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5
